evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
Entity of type FAQPage has no @id — cross-page interconnection blocked
FAQPage schema has 10 Questions but page shows ~22 FAQ pairs
Post is 2219 words (pillar) — include a <nav aria-label="Table of contents"> for LLM outline extraction
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice vs committee 6/10 — Has flashes of voice ('this is the job now') but frequently slips into brand-speak and listicle cadence.
Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — Some good lines ('sand-to-terminal move') mixed with predictable travel-blog phrasing like 'delivers... crowd-pleaser food.'
Quotable sentences 6/10 — A couple of lines are quotable ('WhatsApp is for ranting'), but most sentences are utilitarian.
Intro earns attention 5/10 — Buried under metadata, a stuffed TL;DR, and a typo ('sulting'); the hooky line comes third.

specificity 6/10 — The post names real places and prices throughout (Giovanni's, Matsumoto's, $25 Hanauma Bay fee, $5 Diamond Head), which is strong. But the budget section collapses at the end into a truncated, abstract gesture — 'the stuff nobody remembers paying for' — without completing the examples. The Day 5 kayak section also references 'Kailua Surf & Sea or Enterprise Kailua' but gives no rental price range, leaving a specificity gap in an otherwise cost-conscious post. The worst offender is the cut-off final sentence.
